svg.title: Quantization and distillation — two ways to make LLMs cheaper
svg.desc: Side by side. Left shows quantization: the same model, but weights compressed from 32-bit floats to 8-bit or 4-bit integers, shrinking memory by 4-8x. Right shows distillation: a large teacher model generates soft labels that train a small student model to mimic it, keeping most of the capability at a fraction of the size.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a 32-bit floating point weight in an LLM?')
sendPrompt('How does INT8 quantization work in LLMs?')
sendPrompt('What is 4-bit quantization and how good is it?')
sendPrompt('What is the teacher model in knowledge distillation?')
sendPrompt('What are soft labels in knowledge distillation?')
sendPrompt('How does the student model learn from soft labels?')
sendPrompt('What can a distilled student model do?')
